courses_data.name: BIT Sindri BTech Admission 2026
cutoff_tables: B.Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering
B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering
courses_data.description: BIT Sindri offers a four-year BTech course spread across eight semesters. The institute offers BTech in 10 specialisations, namely Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Production and Industrial Engineering, etc. The institute also offers BTech (LE) for Diploma holders in Engineering. Candidates after completing Class 12 can apply for BIT Sindri BTech programme. Admission to BTech is based on JEE Main score.

faqs.question: What is the requirements at BIT Sindri for BTech?
faqs.answer: Candidates need to fulfil the eligibility criteria to apply for the BIT Sindri BTech programme. It defines the education qualifications required for course admission. Below is a detailed eligibility for BTech:Candidates need to passClass 12standard or equivalent examination from a recognised board.Have studied Mathematics and Physics as compulsory subjects as well as Chemistry or Biology as optional subjects.Have secured a minimum aggregate of 45% in their qualifying exam if they are from the ge

faqs.question: How can I get admission to BTech at BIT Sindri?
faqs.answer: BIT Sindri admission to BTech programme is based on JEE Main score. Candidates selected based on JEE Main score have to come for JEECE Counselling. JEECE counselling is held online. Registered and eligible candidates will have to participate in theJEECE counsellingprocess for admissions into the BTech courses offered by the participating institutes of the state. Since there is no entrance exam, the admissions will be done based on the candidate'sJEE Mainscores.

courses_data.name: BIT Sindri MTech Admission 2026
courses_data.description: BIT Sindri offers two-year MTech programme at the PG level. The institute offers MTech in five major specialisations, namely Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Metallurgical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, etc. Candidates who have completed BE/ BTech or equivalent in respective discipline with a minimum of 60% aggregate or equivalent CGPA (55% aggregate for SC/ ST) can apply for BIT Sindri MTech.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for MTech at BIT Sindri?
faqs.answer: Candidates need to complete BE/ BTech or equivalent in their respective discipline with a minimum of 60% aggregate or equivalent CGPA (55% aggregate for SC/ ST) can apply for BIT Sindri MTech programme. Candidates must hold the documents proving their eligibility for verification at later stages of admission. Besides, aspirants must also qualify GATE or appear for a written exam conducted by BIT.

faqs.question: Which are the MTech specialisations offered at BIT Sindri?
faqs.answer: BIT Sindri offers two-year MTech programme at the PG level. Candidates who have completed graduation are eligible to apply for MTech. The institute offers MTech in five major specialisations, namely:Mechanical EngineeringElectrical EngineeringMetallurgical EngineeringChemical EngineeringCivil Engineering

faqs.question: What is the admission procedure at BIT Sindri?
faqs.answer: BIT Sindri admissions begin with registration. For MTech and PhD, candidates can fill out the application form on the official website of the institute. Candidates are shortlisted for BTech based on JEE Main score. Aspirants applying for BTech have to fill JEECE counselling form. Once the registration is done, aspirants need to appear for JEECE Counselling.GATEscore is considered for admission to M.Tech programme. Aspirants applying for M.Tech can also appear for the entrance exam conducted by the institute. BIT conducts a written test for PhD admission.

faqs.question: How much percentile is required at BIT Sindri?
faqs.answer: Candidates applying for any of the BIT Sindri courses have to fulfill the set eligibility criteria. The eligibility requirement will mention the required qualification and aggregate. Below is BIT Sindri course-wise eligibility:CourseCommon EligibilityBTechClass 12 with Mathematics andPhysicsas compulsory subjects as well as Chemistry or Biology as optional subjects with at least 45% aggregateMTechBE/ BTech or equivalent in respective discipline with a minimum of 60% aggregate or equivalent CGPA (55% aggregate for SC/ ST)PhDME/ M.Tech or equivalent degree in respective discipline with a minimum of 60% aggregate or equivalent (55% aggregate for SC/ ST)

faqs.question: How many marks are necessary for B.Tech. in Civil Engineering at Birsa Institute of Technology, Sindri?
faqs.answer: You need to get more than 95 percentile in JEE-mains. Also, Candidate must have passed 10+2 examination with Physics, Mathematics as compulsory subjects and Chemistry/ Computer Science/ Electronics/ Information Technology/ Biology/ Information Practice/ Biotechnology/ Technical Vocational subjects/ Agricultutre/ Engineering Graphics/ Business Studies/ Entrepreneurship as optional subjects and obtained atleast 45% marks (40% marks in case of candidate belonging to reserved category) in the subjects taken together. For Lateral Entry-Candidate must have passed diploma in relevant field from a recognized board.

placement_report.college_name: BIT Sindri Placements 2025: Highest Package, Average Package, Top Companies

placement_data.title: BIT Sindri Placement Overview 2025
paragraphs: The BIT Sindri has released the placements for season 2025. The highest package stands at INR 16.5 LPA . More than 120 companies took part and made over 560 offers during the BIT Sindri placements 2025.

As per the 2024 placement report, the highest and average package offered during BIT Sindri BTech placements 2024 stood at INR 16.50 LPA and INR 7.57 LPA . Further, the participating recruiters presented a total of 537 job offers and 484 BTech students got placed from the batch of 2024. Meanwhile, 50 top companies took part in the BTech placements 2024. The top recruiters of BIT Sindri placements in 2024 included Capgemini, TATA Steels Ltd, Vedanta, JSW Steel, TATA Power Ltd, Aditya Birla Group, TATA Technologies, AMNS, and L&T, etc.

reviews.review_heading: They have good knowledge, and their behaviour is good.
reviews.review_text: All available facilities are good. Classrooms are well-maintained. Wi-Fi speed is also first-class. Medical facilities are not too good. The canteen staff's behaviour is good, and various sports are available in our college, such as volleyball and cricket.

reviews.review_heading: I am not dissatisfied with the faculty because the faculty in our college is very good.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is very good in our department, such as Wi-Fi, a classroom, and a library. The quality of the hostel in our college is very good and the mess food is also good. Canteens are also available in our college, and the food in the canteen is also very good, such as aloo paratha.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is good for students for their overall development.
reviews.review_text: Labs, classrooms, and libraries are average. Food quality is average but good. Hostel facilities are below average. Hostels are not maintained regularly. Upgradation is needed in this college, especially in labs, as many equipments are not working properly. They should upgrade them.

reviews.review_heading: It has absolutely amazing faculty members and good placements.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is not available, but labs, classrooms, and libraries are very good. All facilities in my hostel are good. The quality of food available in my mess and canteen is very nice and well-cooked. Medical facilities are okay. Sports and games are too good at my college.

reviews.review_heading: Value for money college, placements are good, and the campus is satisfactory.
reviews.review_text: Our college infrastructure is old, many departments have not been maintained for years, but new departments have good infrastructure. Core branches have old labs and infrastructure, but the IT and CS departments are in good condition. The library is good, you can find almost any books of your department, and Wi-Fi is also available. Hostels are of average condition. In 1st year, there were 4 room-sharing partners per room, but in 3rd and final year, most students got a small private room. The ca...

reviews.review_heading: Good placements, helpful faculty, and decent campus life with scope for improvement.
reviews.review_text: BIT, Sindri offers Wi-Fi, well-equipped labs, classrooms with projects, and a large library. Hostels are basic but functional, the mess food is hygienic, and the canteens are affordable. Medical care is available, and sports facilities include cricket, football, volleyball, and indoor games.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely value for money college, with a good alumni network.
reviews.review_text: Our college has state-of-the-art infrastructure as it is the top college under the Government of Jharkhand. Our college has 31 hostels till now, and a new lecture hall complex and auditorium are being made on the campus. The work is almost done; it will be completed in just one more year, and these two will also continue working. The college doesn't provide any sort of Wi-Fi in the hostels, but there are many private operators like Jio and Airtel on campus. You can contact them, but you have to ...

reviews.review_heading: BITians are second to none.
reviews.review_text: Actually, we have a lecture hall, but from this year onwards, the classes will be held in the new lecture hall, which is going to be inaugurated this year, only for the new students. As BIT is one of the oldest colleges in India, its infrastructure is a bit old. About the hostel, it's average, I would say. The food quality is good ( not like the way Hostel food is described), it's genuinely good enough that you can survive. We have a central library in the centre of the college, with a lot of bo...

reviews.review_heading: College is good for your personal studies. You will get enough time to prepare for competition.
reviews.review_text: All branches have separate departments. Bit Sindri has a huge library but the staffs are not that much cooperative. You can take a book for 15 days, and after that, 10 paise/day will be charged. There is no Wi-Fi zone on the campus. Hostel is decent, you have to stay with 4-5 students in the 1st and 2nd year. In 3rd year, you will get separate room.

reviews.review_heading: BIT Sindri: Good faculty, labs, placements; old infrastructure; proactive students thrive here.
reviews.review_text: The campus has decent labs, especially in Civil Engineering. Surveying, Concrete, Soil Mechanics, and Environment labs are well-equipped but sometimes face maintenance delays. The Central Library offers good resources, including textbooks and past papers. Hostels are basic, mostly shared rooms, moderately clean, and old in infrastructure, though some renovations have occurred. Mess food is average with limited variety; canteens provide basic meals. Medical facilities include a dispensary with a ...

reviews.review_heading: Metallurgical branch is a good career option.
reviews.review_text: The 1st-year hostel is clean and spacious, with two students per room, but from 2nd to 3rd year, 3-4 students share a room, and only final-year students get single rooms. Hostel quality declines after the first year. There's no Wi-Fi in hostels or classrooms, only in the library. Labs have old but functional instruments. The mess food is good, offering non-veg meals five days a week. Sports facilities are excellent, with events like SPARDHA, inter-branch football, and intra-branch cricket matche...

reviews.review_heading: If you can't get any of the IIT and NIT also your finance not so good.
reviews.review_text: BIT Sindri's infrastructure has mixed reviews. The college has a large, lush green campus, and separate buildings for its 10 departments. The infrastructure of BIT Sindri is good to moderate but labs are very old, so overall infrastructure is good.

reviews.review_heading: Good faculty members, placements, and infrastructure.
reviews.review_text: All facilities are available like labs, classrooms, and library, and Wi-Fi is in the library. Food in the mess and canteen are good with varieties of menu available, and medical facilities are also there. Sports and games are also prioritized, with a sports club where sports and games are conducted.

reviews.review_heading: The college has an average placement record and good infrastructure for the B.Tech students.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure here is good enough.The classroom and library facilities are good, but you are not provided with Wi-Fi facilities. If I talk about the hostel, it is good for the students, but the mess food is not so good enough. The canteen is really good.

reviews.review_heading: I am satisfied with the faculty and all staff members. Placement varies here.
reviews.review_text: Food is normal and the same as the other colleges. The mess staff members are good. They all are very helpful. If our health is not good, then they also help us. At night or if we go late, then they keep our food.

overview_faqs.question: What is the procedure for withdrawal at VELS University?
overview_faqs.answer: Before Commencement of Program: Application for withdrawal from the programis to be sent to the Admission Office.After Commencement of Program: Application for withdrawal from the programis to be submitted to the Registrar through the respective department. The application willbe processed within 15 days. There shall not be any fee at the time of submittingapplication. A candidate who wishes to withdraw from the Institute after enrolling in the program must obtain a 'No Dues' Certificate from their respective school of study at VISTAS in order to retrieve their certificates.This should be accompanied by the application for withdrawal duly signed by the Parent/Guardian and the original fee receipt and provisional admissionletter ( e-mail request will not be considered ).The certificates will be issued only on production of the 'No-Dues' Certificate in the prescribedform, obtained from the Admissions Office.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Reality of so called reputed university.

content.Placements: Placements are pretty mediocre at their best, only 30 percent of students get placed mainly in software based companies and we do not have proof that we ever have a product based company for placements. The roles offered are pretty basic and only very few get the opportunity for internship.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure is below average. You get pretty slow Wi-Fi in campuses, classrooms are cleaned once a week, medical facilities are not free and they are very expensive if it is an emergency too and the hostel is fine there is nothing too fancy or bad, but the food is bad.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Microbiology - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Satisfied with the faculty, infrastructure could be improved, and good placements last year.

content.Placements: I just joined my batch a few months ago. I don’t know the placement percentage, but there are 63 students enrolled in my course. I also don’t know the package system yet. The recruiting companies for my course are SYASANS and ZOHO, and the top roles are Senior Microbiologist and Lab Technician.
content.Infrastructure: The whole college is divided into particular blocks for certain courses. Some newly built blocks are very comfortable, but the block I am in, the first building made, is honestly not the ideal block to have classrooms in. I come from Lucknow, and it is sort of difficult for me at the start, but now I have adjusted to hostel life. It has been a hard journey, but overall, the hostel is good.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech Electronics and computer Engineering - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: A short review of VELS School of Engineering.

content.Placements: Don't need to worry about placements. VELS is providing good placements, 90% of the students particularly. They have been doing good in this part for the recent few years. VELS is a trusted university. They are offering placements from good companies, salary is not so high but I can say that VELS is one of the top 10 colleges in Chennai that is offering good placements with a decent salary package.

admission_overview.title: VELS University Admission Overview 2026
admission_overview.description: VELS University admission is both entrance and merit-based. The university conducts its own VELS Scholarship Admission Test (V-SAT) to provide scholarship admission to 60+ UG courses. Additionally, the university also accepts national-level entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT, GATE, etc., for admission to MBA and MTech courses, respectively. VELS University offers courses at UG, PG and PhD levels through different schools. The courses are offered in multiple streams like Engineering, Management, Science, Commerce, etc. VELS University admission begins with online registration. Read on the following article to know more details about VELS University course admission.

eligibility_selection.title: VELS University Eligibility & Selection Criteria 2026
eligibility_selection.description: Admission to most UG courses at VELS University is merit-based, considering the marks obtained in Class 12. For PG courses, candidates must have completed their graduation with a minimum of 50% marks. Diploma courses are offered through direct admission, based on the candidate’s academic background. The university also accepts students based on VSAT or national-level exams such as GATE/ CAT/ XAT, etc. Students can check out the following table to know about the general eligibility and selection criteria for VELS University courses: Class 12 with min. 45% -60% from a recognised board

paragraphs: VELS University admission involves application, selection, and admission confirmation. The admission criteria begin with the release of the application form. Check below the step-wiseVELS University admission process:
Application Process
Students can apply online directly through the official application portal of VELS University.
Selection Rounds

VELS University admission 2026 is purely merit-based and is determined by the marks obtained in the 12th standard for the respective courses. However, for law courses, the university also conducts a personal interview as part of the selection process. The university conducts its ownVELS Scholarship Admission Test(V-SAT) to provide scholarship admission to 60+ UG courses. Further, direct admission is offered to BSc and MSc courses based on candidates' scores obtained in the last qualifying exams.
Final Admission
After the completion of all the selection rounds, the selected students may pay a certain amount of course fee to confirm admission.

faqs.question: What is the admission process at Vels University?
faqs.answer: Vels University admission process is inclusive of multiple steps. Candidates need to qualify for each step and proceed for the final selection. Check below the main steps for Vels University admissions:Make InquiryCheck the basic eligiblity requirementsFill the application formComplete the fee payment

faqs.question: How do I get a seat in VELS University?
faqs.answer: VELS is a merit-entrance-based admissions institution. Admission to VELS University requires checking below the process:Application: This has been applied online. Professional course application fee is INR 1, 100. In the process of making the choice, candidates are allowed to apply with the relevant documents.Selection Rounds: The university receives VELS Entrance Exam to take admission to different UG andPG courses. Other than this, CAT and other entrance tests are also accepted by MBA. The university publishes a merit list that gives the names of the shortlisted aspirants.Counselling & Document Checking Round: The short listed candidates are invited to counselling and document checking round. Successful candidates should pay course fee to ensure admission.

faqs.question: Will I get a fee refund if I cancel my LLM admission from VELS University?
faqs.answer: Yes, VELS University does provide a refund in case of withdrawal of admission. However, the refund is subject to fulfillment of certain conditions. Listed below are the guidelines to be followed as per the fee refund policy of the university: If students apply for cancellation prior to the commencement of classes, then they are entitled to a refund of the tuition fees. In such a case, registration and admission fees are not refundable. If a student discontinues in the middle of a course, he/she shall be required to pay the tuition fee for the remaining period of the programme.

faqs.question: What is the School of Hotel and Catering Management, VELS University BSc one-time payment?
faqs.answer: Students need to pay one-time charge of BSc course at School of Hotel and Catering Management, VELS University. This one-time charge can be applicable before enrolment of the course or security charges.

nirf.description:
VELS University has received strong ranks by the NIRF under the 'Overall' category. Provided parameters including, Teaching, Graduation Outcome, Inclusivity and Research, the institute is ranked in a good position as compared to other institutes by the NIRF 2024.
It has been recognized that the institute has displayed stability in trend in the 'University' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2024, VELS University's rank is #101, the same as last year's rank in the 'University' category by the NIRF ranking.
It is to be noticed that the institute has shown a fluctuating trend in the 'Engineering' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2024, VELS University's rank is #101, the same as last year's rank in the 'Engineering' category by the NIRF ranking.
It has been seen that in the 'Pharmacy' category, the institute's ranks have shown a decreasing trend when evaluated against the ranking of the prior year. In 2024, VELS University fell 17 ranks to #64 rank in the 'Pharmacy' category by the NIRF ranking. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.

hostel_campus.college_name: VELS University Chennai Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description:
VISTAS offers residential accommodation for more than 1,000 students within the campus premises. The University houses Ten hostels (AC and NON AC Hostels) 5 Boys Hostel, 3 Girls Hostel a NRI Hostel and an International Students’ Hostel — providing a safe, inclusive, and multicultural living environment.
Rooms are available on a shared basis (2 to 4occupants) with all essential amenities, including air-conditioned options. The hostels have spacious dining halls serving multi-cuisine meals (both vegetarian and non-vegetarian) prepared in hygienic conditions.

library.description:
VELS Institute of Science, Technology and Advanced Studies has a fully automated central library with a seating capacity of 500 persons and follows Open Access System. The library has a collection of 114305 books, 137717 e-books, and 10032 back volumes. It has the subscription of more than 359 Periodicals (National & International), more than 12381 e-journals, 4106 dissertations, 4849 audio-visual resources.
It also facilitates access to e-journals from IEEE/IEL, ASME, ASCE, EBSCO, ProQuest, BENTHAM Science Pharmacy, Micromedex’s Drugdex System, Inventi Online, Nature Online, Law finder, NPTEL etc.
The library is also an associate member of Madras Library Network (MALIBNET) and Institutional member British Council Library.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground, Swimming Pool, Tennis Court, Volleyball Court, Hockey Ground, Cricket Net, Kabaddi Court, Aquatic complex, Laid - out track

reviews.review_heading: Overall, it's very good for students' sake.
reviews.review_text: The atmosphere around the campus, especially for the hostel students studying at our university, is good. The infrastructure is good, and Wi-Fi facility is available for students. They can visit the library after class hours at any time, as it remains open until 8:00 PM. The university provides all necessary facilities and shows extra involvement in sports activities for the benefit of students.

reviews.review_heading: Best university life is in VELS University.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi, labs, gyms, cafeteria, library, auditorium: we have all the facilities with good amenities. The medical facilities are spot on and very immediate in action. The sports clubs are one of the best. It has both indoor and outdoor activities. The quality of food is very easily adaptable.

reviews.review_heading: Good for aviation - overall average.
reviews.review_text: Our college's outdated infrastructure and poor maintenance make for an unpleasant experience. The bathrooms, in particular, are unsanitary and emit strong odors that can be suffocating. This, combined with other shortcomings, creates a less-than-ideal living environment for students residing in the hostel.

reviews.review_heading: VISTAS- facts for future aspects.
reviews.review_text: They have an average hostel and other infrastructure. The foods provided in the hostel will be fair to eat. All the students can have Wi-Fi access and library activities to get their knowledge in the hostel also. They have a safe and secured hostel in the college.

reviews.review_heading: Overall, it is a good college with a nice environment.
reviews.review_text: In our department, we have all lab facilities, but currently, the simulator lab is under construction, and we don't have Wi-Fi facilities in college. The hostels here have Wi-Fi facilities and 24-hour water service. The food here is average, but the rooms are comfortable and neat.

reviews.review_heading: Our college provides good infrastructure and many events.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is good. Simulator lab is available to gain knowledge of aircraft. They arrange some industrial visits that are very helpful to gain knowledge about the aircraft and applicable in the aviation department.

reviews.review_heading: VELS University, B.Sc. Biotechnology.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi facility and labs were good for my course. The library was good and easily accessible for most students on campus. The hostel food was good at the start of the year, I won’t talk about the middle of the year. I mostly ordered food from outside, and by the end of the year, it was super good. The hostel rooms were okay. There was laundry service and vending machines for snacks and juices. Wi-Fi facility was good in hostels.

reviews.review_heading: Yeah, now they are a little busy with their classes, but they are good in teaching.
reviews.review_text: In our course, there is Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, and a library—all well maintained and providing good infrastructure for students. They have a lab assistant for all types of lab work, and they provide good teaching in practicals.

reviews.review_heading: It's overall a good college with good environment.
reviews.review_text: We have good infrastructures. With all Lab facilities and library( closed due to flood damage) And hostel has AC and non AC , with wifi facility . Food in hostel are so average. The campus is fully guarded with bouncers and security.and they have parking facility

reviews.review_heading: The placement was increased by 2.5% in 2019.
reviews.review_text: The facilities offered by the college for the students include air conditioners in classes as well as in hostels. The classroom is made according to the convenience of every student. Projectors have been operational in each classroom. The quality of the food, as well as the hostel rooms, are comfortable as well. Talking about the library, we have a 3-floor building in which we can find every type of book related to various topics and courses.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied because of some faculty members.
reviews.review_text:
Wi-Fi was good. Labs facilities were excellent but classrooms were just okay.
Library facilities were marvellous. 
Food was okay
Hostel Rooms were not that much good.
For medical emergency Apollo Hospital was available. 
Sports were good.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied because of faculty, really good for learn more things and parts about life.
reviews.review_text: My department has free Wi-Fi, labs, and smart classroom facilities. The hostel food is not bad, but it is good to eat. Some foods, like poori, are not as good, but no one can complain about it because the food is not bad in flavor and taste. It is lower in cost compared to homemade food, but it's okay. The entire campus is very clean.

VELS University also conducts faculty and student exchange programmes.

MBA students can become Brand Managers, Sales Managers, B2B Sales Managers, Digital Marketing Managers, and others. The total seat intake for MBA students is 90. IGDTUW Delhi students are taught communication, presentation, and various other skills.
Overall, IGDTUW Delhi is one of the best universities offering quality education to students. faqs.answer: A:    AIMA MAT releases the results phase wise. The exam is held in multiple sessions in February, May, September and December phases. So the results are released for all the sessions of a particular phase on the same day. The MAT February result of all sessions are released in March. The MAT May results are released in June and the MAT September result is released in October and MAT December results are released in January. Candidates who appear for the exam, can download their scorecard for their exam session from the AIMA MAT website. AIMA does not release cut offs for admission through MAT. It has no role in the MAT cut off determination. The participating institutes determine MAT cut offs on their own and as per their selection parameters and criteria.

review_content.General: The college does not have a huge campus area, but it is fine. Washrooms, rooms, ground, and labs are cleaned, proper lighting, AC rooms, titles over the floor and walls. Wi-Fi works well, the library is also well-maintained, and different types of books are available. However, the condition of the hostel is not very good, as the building is old, but the management tries to do everything to make it comfortable for the students. Mess food is also fine, the canteen menu is not too lengthy, but momos and spring rolls are tasty for sure.

Even before placement, all the companies visit this college to guide students and help them to prepare for the interview and the selection process, and it is called pre-placement talks, which definitely gives college students an edge over those students who are appearing from outside the college.
review_content.Infrastructure: The hostel is average. They provide basics like AC, decent food, decent water to drink, and basic rooms are small, washing machines are 2 per washroom, but not all of them work, but some do, which teaches you patience. faqs.answer: General, OBC, SC, ST, or PwD categories have different cutoff ranks for admission.In any case, with a 88% score in JEE Fundamental, you have areas of strength for an of getting into IGDTUW for a few B.Tech programs.A 88% score in JEE Principal ordinarily means a percentile somewhere in the range of 95 and 98, which is an extremely serious score.If you belong to OBC, SC, ST, or PwD categories, the cutoff ranks for you will be significantly lower, further increasing your chances of admission.
faqs.question: Q: Can I get IGDTUW, B.Tech with 88% in JEE Main?

faqs.answer: After JEE session 2 is completed, only those candidates who are aiming for IITs need to start preparing for JEE Advanced after clearing JEE Main. Those who are above the 98th percentile should start preparing for JEE Advanced as soon as possible. Those below this, between the 95th and 98th percentile, should focus on attempting more mock tests and previous year question papers so that they have a better chance of clearing JEE Advanced

overview_faqs.question: Is MBA from IGDTUW worth it?
overview_faqs.answer: Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW) is recognised by the UGC and is a member of the Association of Indian Universities. It has been ranked 161 among the best Engineering colleges in India by NIRF and seventh-best Engineering colleges in Delhi by NIRF 2021. The MBA course is offered for four years across multiple specialisations, including Finance, Human Resource Management, Marketing, Business Analytics, etc. The overall fees for MBA is around INR 1.6 lakh. Talking about the placemenst report 2022, 304 out of 396 UG (4-year) and PG (2-year) students were placed during Indira Gandhi Delhi University for Women placements 2021. The median package offered to UG (4-year) and PG (2-year) students was INR 15 LPA and INR 10 LPA during Indira Gandhi Technical University placements 2021, respectively.

review_summary.college_name: IGDTUW Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: My own IGDTUW experience.

content.Placements: Almost 70%-80% of students are placed in internships for the third year. The top roles offered are SDE intern, Adapt intern, etc. Top recruiting companies are Wells Fargo and ServiceNow. The highest package, I guess, is 1.5 LPA for an intern, Rs. 45,000 minimum.
content.Infrastructure: There is a Wi-Fi connection, and it works well. Overall, the classrooms are decent and air-conditioned, and the library is also pretty good. The quality of facilities in the hostel is good. They provide medical facilities, a mess, and everything. My favourite spot is the library.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Good placement, good infrastructure (not as good as other colleges).

content.Placements: Around 70% students, who were eligible to sit in placements, got placed last year. The highest package is around 50 LPA, and the lowest can be expected as 3 LPA-4 LPA. The off-campus placement has even offered a 1 crore per annum package to a student. Internships are offered only to CSE students most of the time. However, the average stipend is around Rs. 30,000, but not all the students get internships on campus. The role you get solely depends on your skill level and your interview.
content.Infrastructure: The college does not have a huge campus area, but it is fine. Washrooms, rooms, ground, and labs are cleaned, proper lighting, AC rooms, titles over the floor and walls. Wi-Fi works well, the library is also well-maintained, and different types of books are available. However, the condition of the hostel is not very good, as the building is old, but the management tries to do everything to make it comfortable for the students. Mess food is also fine, the canteen menu is not too lengthy, but momos and spring rolls are tasty for sure.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Reviewing my college IGDTUW.

content.Placements: The placement is very good here, and many companies visit this college. Even before placement, all the companies visit this college to guide students and help them to prepare for the interview and the selection process, and it is called pre-placement talks, which definitely gives college students an edge over those students who are appearing from outside the college.
content.Infrastructure: The hostel is average. They provide basics like AC, decent food, decent water to drink, and basic rooms are small, washing machines are 2 per washroom, but not all of them work, but some do, which teaches you patience. The hostel timings are very strict. You have to be in the hostel till 8:00 PM, but if you want to go to the college computer lab, then you can stay there till 11:00 PM.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: IGDTUW offers a balanced college experience with scope to grow if you use opportunities well.

content.Placements: For batch 2024, B.Tech CSE at IGDTUW, 77% of students got placed, with the highest package of 56 LPA, and the average package is 25.15 LPA. Internships are secured by most third & fourth-year students via companies like Microsoft, Adobe, JP Morgan, etc., with roles mostly in software dev, backed, cloud, and data analysis.
content.Infrastructure: IGDTUW has decent Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, and a library. The hostel is basic with average mess food, the canteen is okay, medical help is limited, and sports facilities exist but need better upkeep. Overall, the infrastructure is functional but not exceptional.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electronics and Communications Engineering (Artificial Intelligence) - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: College has good placements with a decent curriculum. But the infrastructure needs improvement.

content.Placements: Placements for CS and related branches at IGDTUW are outstanding, while for ECE, ECE AI, and MAE, they are decent. About 50% of students are getting placed. The average package is 18 LPA, with the highest at 1 crore (MotorQ) and the lowest is 4 LPA - 5 LPA. In ECE, the highest is 56 LPA (Adobe). Top recruiters include Google, Microsoft, Adobe, and Morgan Stanley, with internships at Google and BNY Mellon. Top role offered is SDE.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure at IGDTUW is satisfactory but has scope for improvement. Wi-Fi works well in the academic blocks but is inconsistent in hostels and some areas. Labs are functional but could be better equipped. Classrooms in ECE and CSE generally lack air-conditioning, which can be challenging during peak summers. Library provides good resources but often falls short on seating capacity during exams. Campus has a spacious ground for sports and activities. Although I haven't stayed in the hostel, friends share that the food quality is decent and the rooms, though slightly small, are manageable.

admission_overview.college_name: IGDTUW Course Admissions 2025: Dates, Application Process, Fee

paragraphs: Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW)admission 2025is PG courses will open soon. Candidates can visit the official website to enquire about the admission procedure. Btecg and BArch admission/ counselling throughJoint Admission Committee (JAC)is ongoing for 2025.GDTUWis awomen-only technical universitythat offers technical and management courses. Some of the popular courses offered by the certified university are BTech, BBA, MBA, MCA, BArch, etc.IGDTUWadmission to the offered courses is based onnational-level entrance exams. The university also offers PhD in direct and regular mode. Moreover, candidates shall check the eligibility conditions for the course before the form is filled out.

IGDTUW eligibility for the UG and PG programmes is mainly the last qualifying exam candidates have passed. As per the university's admission guidelines, candidates must meet the eligibility criteria of the course and also the selection criteria. The selection criteria are mostly based on national-level entrance exams and counselling. Find below the course-wise eligibility and selection criteria of IGDTUW:
*This information is sourced from the official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

IGDTUW fees range crouse-wise from INR 57,000 to INR 8.6 lakh. Admission is completed once the tuition fee is paid. Additionally, extra charges might be included during the admission process such as student welfare, examination fees, development funds, etc. Tabulated below are course-wise fees for 2025:
*This information is sourced from the official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

IGTUW offers a limited number of seats for its UG, PG, and PhD programme. For BTech and BArch seat allotment is done viaJoint Admission Counseling (JAC)and the rest of the course's seat allotment is done via the provisional list released by the university. Tabulated below are course-wise IGTUW seat intake for 2025:
*This information is sourced from the official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

Candidates willing to take admission at IGDTUW must complete the admission process as per the university's admission guidelines. IGDTUW admission process is a multi-step process which is different for every other course. Candidates must make sure to read carefully the admission process for their desired course. Find below the IGTUW admission process for 2025:
Step 1: Application Process 2025

Candidates must first register themselves to begin the application process. BTech/BArch programme's admission is done via the Joint Admission Counseling (JAC). Therefore candidates must visit the official website of JAC and register there by providing all the required details. For, BBA/ MBA candidates must apply through CMAC's official website. Further, for the rest PG and PhD programme candidates must apply via the IGTUW's official website.
Step 2: Selection Process

Candidate's applications are scrutinised for the selection process. For, BTech JEE Main's score is considered for selection and BBA/BArch programme's selection is done based on the last qualifying exam.MAT, CAT, and GATEexams are considered for the MBA/MTech/MPlan programme selection. For MCA the selection is done based onCUETconducted by the university and finally for the PhD selection is done based on theXAT RATfollowed by an interview.
Step 3: Merit list/Seat allotment

UG candidates must register for the JAC counselling for seat allotment. Based on the selection process the university releases a provisional list with candidates' names who have qualified for the selection round.
Step 4: Final selection
Once steps 1, 2, and 3 are completed seats are allotted as per the admission/ counselling bodies. Candidates must pay the admission fee to confirm the seat.

faqs.question: What is the admission process for BTech at IGDTUW?
faqs.answer: Admission to the BTech course at IGDTUW is through JEE Main scores, followed by counselling of JAC Delhi. The candidates should have passed Class 12 with a minimum of 60% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. Seat allotment is done on the basis of JEE Main rank, category, and availability. Candidates should register on the JAC Delhi website and go through the counselling procedure.

faqs.question: Does IGDTUW accept any entrance exam for MBA admission?
faqs.answer: Yes,Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW)accepts CAT scores for MBA admission. While admission to the MBA will strictly be dependent on the candidate's performance in the CAT followed by GD and PI, admission to the MCA is conducted by an entrance test conducted by IGDTUW.Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW)conducts a Research Aptitude Test (RAT) for doctoral candidates, followed by an interview.

faqs.question: Is MAT 2026 registration open for admission to MBA courses at GIBS Business School?
faqs.answer: No, MAT registration 2026 are now closed. The last date for PBT registration was February 23, 2026 and for CBT Registration was March 2, 2026.  GIBS Business School, like many other institutions, accepts MAT scores for MBA admissions.

faqs.question: Will I get direct admission in IGDTUW with 85% in Class 12?
faqs.answer: For merit-based programs such as BBA and BArch,  85% in Class 12 can get admission at IGDTUW. But BTech, MTech, MCA, and MBA need entrance exams, and Class 12 marks will not be enough. Admission is through competitive exams such as JEE Main, GATE, and CAT. It is advisable to verify course-wise eligibility before applying.

faqs.answer: IGDTUW admission to its UG and PG programme is based on the entrance exam conducted by the state and national level examination bodies. Also for few course entrance exams are conducted by the university itself. faqs.question: What can students do after JEE Main session 2 result?
faqs.answer: After JEE session 2 is completed, only those candidates who are aiming for IITs need to start preparing for JEE Advanced after clearing JEE Main. Those who are above the 98th percentile should start preparing for JEE Advanced as soon as possible. Those below this, between the 95th and 98th percentile, should focus on attempting more mock tests and previous year question papers so that they have a better chance of clearing JEE Advanced

placement_report.college_name: IGDTUW Placement & Salary Insights

placement_data.title: IGDTUW Placement Overview 2024
paragraphs: IGDTUW has a placement cell that is responsible for securing lucrative career opportunities for students. The institute invites companies from different sectors to conduct the placement process at the Campus premises.

As per the latest report, IGDTUW recorded 69.3% placements for the Class of 2024. A total of 135 recruiters participated during the IGDTUW placement in 2024 and placed 537 students of the 2024 batch. The participating recruiters placed 56 CSE AI students, 145 CSE, 125 IT, 95 ECE and 64 MAE students of the BTech batch 2024. Additionally, the MCA batch 2024 consists of 74 students, of whom 49 were placed with handsome packages. The highest and average package offered to the BTech CSE batch 2024 stood at INR 56 LPA and INR 25.15 LPA , respectively. Further, the highest and average package offered to the MCA students during IGDTUW placements 2024 stood at INR 52.89 LPA and INR 14.13 LPA , respectively.

Apart from offering placement opportunities, IGDTUW also offers internship opportunities to the students of the Class 2024. According to the report, 427 students received internship offers from reputed organizations. The top recruiters who participated during IGDTUW placement in 2024 included Google, Adobe, Palo Alto, Microsoft, Uber, Atlassian, BNY Mellon, Deloitte, JP Morgan, Cisco, PWC, Salesforce, Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, British Telecom, etc.
Download : IGDTUW Placement Report 2024

description: IGDTUW cutoff 2025 is out for BTech course specialisations. Students can refer to the exam-wise sections below to view the IGDTUW JAC Delhi cutoff 2025 across different specialisations, rounds and categories. Further updates are below:

IGDTUW cutoff 2024 was released for admission to the BTech and BArch courses across various specialisations. The JEE Main cutoff 2024 for IGDTUW concluded after Round 5 of the Seat Allotment results for General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, and PWD categories. During the IGDTUW JEE Main cutoff 2024, the Last Round closing ranks ranged between 2490 and 42614, wherein Bachelor of Architecture (B.Arch.) was the toughest course to get into the Delhi Technological University for the students belonging to the Ge

IGDTUW last three years' JEE Main cutoff data is available for Engineering courses across various specialisations. Considering the last round closing rank cutoff over the past two years, BTech in CSE remains the most competitive branch among the General AI category students. On the contrary, BTech in Mechanical and Automation Engineering has lost its charm over the years, as the closing ranks have drastically increased. This indicates that the course is no longer in demand among the students.

JEE Main counselling has been concluded for DTU admission to the BE/ BTech and Integrated BTech courses for 2024. For the next academic session, the JEE Main 2025 schedule is yet to be released. In the meantime, aspirants can refer to the table below to check out the JEE Main tentative schedule.

The most sought-after BTech specialisation among the General HS category candidates is Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence) with a Round 5 closing rank of 47534. On the other hand, Mechanical and Automation Engineering + Master of Business Administration (MBA) turned out to be the leas

Along with the Round 5 cutoff for 2025, students can also find the last round cutoff lists for the previous years to have insights into previous years' cutoff trends. The Round 5 cutoff ranks varied between 47534 and 137809 among the General Home State female category students. Considering the cutoff ranks, the top three most competitive courses for admission are Bachelor of Arch

hostel_campus.college_name: IGDTUW Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description: The University has two women hostels - Krishna Hostel and Kaveri Hostel. All rooms are on twin/triple sharing basis and are equipped with individual beds, chairs, built-in cupboards and study tables. In addition to this, IGDTUW Hostels provide purified drinking water, housekeeping services, wi-fi facility, news paper/ magazines, TV, Washing machines, facilities of hot water for residents, paid laundry services etc. The residents use the campus play ground for games

library.description: Learning Resource Centre of IGDTUW, Delhi has a highly selective collection of print, electronic, and audiovisual materials in the areas of science, engineering, technology and management to support the learning and research activities of students.The Digital Library section has e-materials like CDs, DVDs and digital thesis.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Skating

reviews.review_heading: My own IGDTUW experience.
reviews.review_text: There is a Wi-Fi connection, and it works well. Overall, the classrooms are decent and air-conditioned, and the library is also pretty good. The quality of facilities in the hostel is good. They provide medical facilities, a mess, and everything. My favourite spot is the library.

reviews.review_heading: Good placement, good infrastructure (not as good as other colleges).
reviews.review_text: The college does not have a huge campus area, but it is fine. Washrooms, rooms, ground, and labs are cleaned, proper lighting, AC rooms, titles over the floor and walls. Wi-Fi works well, the library is also well-maintained, and different types of books are available. However, the condition of the hostel is not very good, as the building is old, but the management tries to do everything to make it comfortable for the students. Mess food is also fine, the canteen menu is not too lengthy, but momo...

reviews.review_heading: Reviewing my college IGDTUW.
reviews.review_text: The hostel is average. They provide basics like AC, decent food, decent water to drink, and basic rooms are small, washing machines are 2 per washroom, but not all of them work, but some do, which teaches you patience. The hostel timings are very strict. You have to be in the hostel till 8:00 PM, but if you want to go to the college computer lab, then you can stay there till 11:00 PM.

reviews.review_heading: IGDTUW offers a balanced college experience with scope to grow if you use opportunities well.
reviews.review_text: IGDTUW has decent Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, and a library. The hostel is basic with average mess food, the canteen is okay, medical help is limited, and sports facilities exist but need better upkeep. Overall, the infrastructure is functional but not exceptional.

reviews.review_heading: College has good placements with a decent curriculum. But the infrastructure needs improvement.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure at IGDTUW is satisfactory but has scope for improvement. Wi-Fi works well in the academic blocks but is inconsistent in hostels and some areas. Labs are functional but could be better equipped. Classrooms in ECE and CSE generally lack air-conditioning, which can be challenging during peak summers. Library provides good resources but often falls short on seating capacity during exams. Campus has a spacious ground for sports and activities. Although I haven't stayed in the hostel, f...

reviews.review_heading: If you want placement then definitely go for it.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is like average because only few classes have AC in them. The campus is very small. Hostel is not good at all. Lab classes have AC but others are non-AC. But in 2025 IT block will have AC in all the rooms in which the classes for CS, AI, IT, and AIML take place.

reviews.review_heading: Great placement opportunities.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college is average. You don't need to walk a lot to get to your classes like other colleges. Facilities are not up to expectations. You don't have a proper seating arrangement to have your food in the canteen. We do have Nescafé where you can enjoy yourself. The campus is shared by the students of Dr B. R. Ambedkar. The sports societies are active. Most of the computers don't work in the labs, and the library gets crowded most of the time.

reviews.review_heading: Good exposure and opportunities, a supportive environment, and improving placements.
reviews.review_text: The overall infrastructure of IGDTUW is average. Wi-Fi is available across the campus but not always reliable. Labs are equipped for basic requirements, though some equipment is outdated. Classrooms are spacious with projectors. The library is useful with good resources. Hostel facilities are manageable, with rooms of decent size and average cleanliness. The mess food is also average, sometimes good, sometimes just okay, with better options available in the canteen. Medical facilities are basic....

reviews.review_heading: Strong placement and supportive faculty, but infrastructure needs improvement.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure of the institute is below average when compared to the top government colleges in India. The library often remains overcrowded making it difficult for students to study comfortably. Classrooms are not fully centralized. In addition, Wi-Fi connectivity is restricted to only a few specific labs, limiting accessibility across the campus. The quality of hostel food also falls short of expectations and requires significant improvement.

reviews.review_heading: Great placements and campus.
reviews.review_text: The overall infrastructure of the campus is well maintained and student friendly. The academic facilities include spacious classrooms with modern teaching aids, fully equipped labs and well stocked library with access to online journals. Hostel facilities are quite comfortable providing all the basic needs. The mess food is hygienic and offers a variety of food.

reviews.review_heading: I am truly satisfied with my faculty, college life, friends, and the new experiences I have gained.
reviews.review_text: The IT department is newly built and has smart boards in almost every class. Wi-Fi can only be accessed on the 1st floor of IT and the library. Classrooms are good and spacious, but still sometimes congested. The library is well-managed and has lots of books related to our course. I am not a hosteler, so I don't know about it.

reviews.review_heading: Overall, this college is really good.
reviews.review_text: They have AC only in the IT department and in labs. Food quality is average, like any other college. Hostel is not that good, they provide AC only to 1st years, plus you have bunkers bed in first year, like 6 people sharing a room for AC. In others, there are triple, so overall hostel is not that good.

reviews.review_heading: My review of this college.
reviews.review_text: Hostel facilities are nice, only two students have to share the room. 24/7 Wi-Fi facility in the hostel, as well as water and electricity facility, is also minimal. The hostel mess food is also good and hygienic as it is checked every weekend, and there are timing restrictions from 9:00 PM onwards, nobody is allowed.

reviews.review_heading: Good placement in 2025 and in the ECE-AI department. Infrastructure should be improved.
reviews.review_text: The ECE-AI department at IGDTUW has an average environment. They provide good and well-equipped labs with Wi-Fi facilities. There is a decent library. Some facilities are a bit outdated. I'm a day scholar, but one of my best friend is a hosteller, she told me about hostel that it is just poor, like the food is so bad and tasteless, rooms are so small, and the rules and regulations are so strict. The medical facilities available are below average. The classrooms are not fully air-conditioned in E...

reviews.review_heading: Review of Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW).
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure are quite decent, with well-maintained classrooms equipped with AC. Currently, the only exception is the mechanical branch, which doesn't have AC yet. The hostel food used to be exceptionally tasty. Even though I am a day scholar, I often eat with my friends in the hostel because the food is genuinely delicious.

data.description: IGDTUW has experienced and professional faculty members with years of experience. Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women faculty motivates students to work on their knowledge and skills. Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW Delhi) faculty are PhD, diploma, and degree holders.

review_summarisation.heading: What students say about Indira Gandhi Delhi Technical University for Women (IGDTUW)

likes.text: The top companies are Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Uber, Morgan Stanley, Philips, etc., visit our campus for recruitment.
Students of CS and IT branches get more internships as compared to ECE and MAE.

At the PG level, the university offers MBA, MSc, MCA, and various other courses to students. GJUST-Guru Jambheshwar University of Science And Technology fee structure gets renewed every year by the authority. As per the 2025 fee structure, the ranges from INR 5,400 to 3,06,000. After final selection, the fee payment must be done for seat confirmation. The university also provides PhD and various other courses to students. faqs.answer: A:    Yes, no entrance exam score is required for GJUST BPharm course. Hence, you can get admission without any entrance test scores. The aspirants are admitted based on their aggregate in the last qualifying examination, i.e., Class 12 or equivalent. Candidates who meet the course-specific eligibility criteria can proceed with the application formalities. GJUST BPharm application can be filled out in online mode by visiting the university official website. Application must be submitted before the deadline. The university then scans applications for the eligibility fulfilment and selection. Shortlisted students have to take part in counselling for seat allotment. The seat can finally be confirmed on payment of admission fee.
faqs.question: Can I get admission at GJUST to BPharm course without an entrance exam?

faqs.answer: A:    A pass in Class 12 with at least 50% aggregate (47.5% aggregate for SC candidates for Haryana) is required to apply for GJUST BA (Hons) course. Candidates must hold the documents proving their eligibility for admission. Additionally, aspirants also need to qualify GJUST Entrance Test for admission to BA (Hons) course. The university releases a merit list announcing the names of the shortlisted candidates. Selected aspirants are further called for a counselling round.

review_content.Placements: The placements are even less than what we call decent here. You cant expect more from the on campus placements from this college. review_content.Infrastructure: The hostels of the university are in a way like, like you get a hostel in just 1st year, after that, there will be no hostels facility for students and still the hostels are not that good. 4 to5 students live in a single room and it becomes impossible for someone to study.

overview_faqs.question: What is the total fee for GJUST BPharm course?
overview_faqs.answer: GJUST BPharm fees includes various components, such as caution money, tuition fee, hostel fee, and more. Some of these components are one-time chargeable, like caution deposit. While tuition fee and other such components have to be paid annually/semesterly. GJUST BPharm tuition fee is INR 3,400, while the hostel charges are INR 25,400 and one-time fee is of INR 2,000.At the time of enrollment, students need to pay the admission fee. It must be noted that the mentioned fee amount is taken from the official website/sanctioning body. This is subject to change. Hence, is indicative.

overview_faqs.question: Is GJUST a good college?
overview_faqs.answer: Guru Jambheshwar University of Science & Technology is one of the esetmeed university which offers quality education to the students. The university is approved by UGC and AICTE, accredited by NBA, and with an A+ Grade by NAAC. The university is ranked 49 under the Pharmacy category by NIRF 2023. The the highest package offered during GJU Hisar placements 2023 was INR 14.75 LPA.

overview_faqs.question: Is MBA expensive at GJUST?
overview_faqs.answer: The total fee for GJUST MBA course is 1.37 lakh. The fee is calculated based on annual fee and excludes university charges. Check below the fee break-up for GJUST MBParticularsFeeAdmission/ continuation fee1,800Development fund5,800Tuition fee29,000Examination fee2,600Other charges29,600Total fee per annum68,800

review_summary.college_name: GJUST Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Facilities are average, like infrastructure, campus life, crowd, teachers, and hostel.

content.Placements: The highest package from CSE is 6 LPA, and the average package is around 3 LPA. 5-10 students are generally placed till now (mid placement season). Most of the students try to find internships off campus by building their skills on their own, and mostly get their placements off campus.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, library are good. There are all the facilities for a studious student who genuinely wants to study. But the hostel provided for B.Tech students is very bad. No space in the room, no separate almirah, table, chair, or even bed. The food is also very bad at this hostel.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPT) - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely dissatisfied because of the infrastructure. The faculty is supportive and caring.

content.Placements: No placements are available in my course at my college. But it gives placements in internships, which are very few. They are given to about 2-3 students in a full batch only, which is a very bad thing. I am absolutely dissatisfied with it, and it should give placements for jobs also.
content.Infrastructure: Benches in classrooms are bad and broken. Fans are not working properly. Hostels are average, and the food is average. The good thing about the college is that it has a very large sports complex, which has many indoor game courts and outdoor sports as well. There is a good library which is very large, and seats are available in very good condition.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electronics and Computer Engineering - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Review from 2nd sem B.Tech E&C student.

content.Placements: The placements are even less than what we call decent here. You cant expect more from the on campus placements from this college. They roam around 3 to 4 LPA as the average package. Of course, it depends on the crowd as well, but still, there are very few packages offered here.
content.Infrastructure: The hostels of the university are in a way like, like you get a hostel in just 1st year, after that, there will be no hostels facility for students and still the hostels are not that good. 4 to5 students live in a single room and it becomes impossible for someone to study.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: GJU, Hisar, college life review.

content.Placements: In our university, almost 50 percent of the students are placed on the campus. The average package is 4 LPA, and the highest package is 23 LPA. Some of the companies that are enrolled in our university are Wipro, Infosys, TVS, etc. Data scientist, data analytics, and AI engineering are the top courses seen in placement and internship.
content.Infrastructure: There is very good infrastructure that helps us to learn easily. Very good classrooms and smart classes with Wi-Fi for each student are provided in the hostel. The food quality is not very good, but it has good medical facilities and a good sports ground.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Packaging Technology - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: A great place for practical learning in printing & packaging.

content.Placements: Placements in the printing & packaging technology course are decent but still improving. Around 60-70% students get placed every year. Top recruiting companies include ITC, Parksons Packaging, Zircon, TCPL, and Natraj. The highest package goes around 7-8 LPA, the average is around 3-4 LPA, and the lowest can be 2-2.5 LPA. Many students also get internships in corrugation, label printing and flexible packaging companies, which helps in gaining industry exposure. More core printing companies are expected to come in future.
content.Infrastructure: The university has a large campus with greenery and well-maintained buildings. The printing department is equipped with modern labs, offset and flexographic printing machines, computer labs, and a good library. Wi-Fi is available, and classrooms are clean and spacious. Hostel facilities are good, rooms are maintained properly, and the mess food is average but hygienic. Sports facilities, medical centre, canteen and stationery shops are available on campus.

admission_overview.college_name: GJUST Course Admissions 2025: Last date to apply, Selection, Fees & Dates

admission_overview.title: GJUST Admission Overview 2025
admission_overview.description: GJUST admissions are both merit and entrance-based. The university conducts an entrance exam to shortlist candidates for various UG and PG courses. Apart from this, GJUST accepts national-level entrance exams like JEE Main, CAT, etc. for admission to specific courses. The university offers more than 50 courses at UG and PG levels in multiple streams such as Engineering, Management, Science, Arts, etc. in campus and through affiliated colleges. GJUST also has a Directorate of Distance Education to offer UG and PG courses in distance education mode.

eligibility_selection.title: GJUST Eligibility & Selection 2025
eligibility_selection.description: The university conducts an entrance exam to shortlist candidates for courses such as BA, MA, B.Ed, etc. For some of the programmes, national-level entrance exams such as JEE Main, CAT, etc. Given below is the list of popular GJUST courses along with their selection and eligibility criteria to apply for GJUST Hisare course admissions:

courses_table.eligibility: Class 12
Graduation with at least 50% aggregate (47.5% aggregate for SC candidates for Haryana)
Class 12 with at least 50% aggregate (47.5% aggregate for SC candidates for Haryana)

Bachelor's degree and/ or master’s degree in Sciences/ Social Sciences/ Humanity with at least 50% aggregate Bachelor’s in Engineering or Technology with specialisation in Science and Mathematics with a minimum of 55% aggregate or any other qualification equivalent thereto
Graduation in relevant subjects with at least 55% aggregate
Any Commerce graduate/ BA with Economics/ BBA or equivalent with at least 50% aggregate (47.5% aggregate for SC candidates of Haryana

faqs.answer: It is necessary that all the aspirants meet the course-specific eligibility criteria. Hence, it must be checked out before the form filling. Aspirants need to pass Class 12 to apply for BTech course. Check below detailed GJUST BTech eligibility requirements:An applicant should have passed Class 12 examination with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects along with Chemistry/ Bio-Technology/ Biology/ Technical Vocational subject.For admission to BE/ BTech (Bio-Technology), a candidate should be a pass in Class 12 examination with Physics and Mathematics or Biology as compulsory subjects along with Chemistry/ Bio-Technology/ Computer Science.A candidate who is passed in Diploma in Engineering and Technology from a recognised institute/ university.Candidates should have obtained 45% marks (40% in case of reserved category candidates).Note: In the absence of information, the above eligibility is taken from HSTES Counselling.
faqs.question: What are the essential qualifications for admission to BTech course at GJUST?

faqs.answer: The eligibility criteria varies from one BSc specialisation to another. It varies in terms of subject combination and the required minimum aggregate in the qualifying exam. The GJUST BSc eligibility criteria for some courses is mentioned in the table below:CourseEligibility CriteriaIntegrated BSc (Physical Sciences) + MSc MathematicsClass 12 pass with a minimum of 60% aggregate (57% for SC candidates of Haryana) in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics subjects taken together from a recognised boardBSc NursingThe age limit is 17 years as on Dec 31 and the maximum age limit is 35 years. Candidate should have passed Class 12 with at least 45% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology taken together. Additionally, applicant must have pass marks in English. Candidate should be medically fit. Married candidates can also apply and blind candidates are also eligible.BSc (H) with Research + MSc Yoga Science and TherapyClass 12 or equivalent pass in any discipline with at least 50% aggregate (47.5% for SC candidates of Haryana) from a recognised board.
faqs.question: What is the required qualification for BSc admission at GJUST?

faqs.answer: A pass in Class 12 with at least 50% aggregate (47.5% aggregate for SC candidates for Haryana) is required to apply for GJUST BA (Hons) course. Candidates must hold the documents proving their eligibility for admission. Additionally, aspirants also need to qualify GJUST Entrance Test for admission to BA (Hons) course. The university releases a merit list announcing the names of the shortlisted candidates. Selected aspirants are further called for a counselling round.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility for GJUST BA (Hons) admission?

paragraphs: GJUST admission processbegins with registration for the course of their choice. The selection will be entrance-based for most of the programme. Check below step-wise GJUST admission process:
1. Application Process
Eligible students can apply online. You need to pay application fee to complete the process.
2. Selection

Once the candidates have applied for the course of their choice, their applications are evaluated. The selection for most of the programmes such as BA, MA, MBA, etc. is based onGJUST Entrance Test. National-level entrance exams are also accepted for admission such asJEE Main for BTechandCAT for MBA.For others, merit in the last qualifying exam is considered for admission.
3. Counselling and Seat Allotment

Once the selection rounds are over, shortlisted candidates are called for counselling. BTech candidates have to appear for HSTES Counselling. Aspirants are required to lock their preferences. The list of seat allotment will be available on the official website of the university. Selected candidates need to pay programme fee to confirm their seat in the university.

GJUST fee includes multiple components such as tuition, caution deposit, hostel charges, etc. Some of the components are one-time paid while others are paid annually. Candidates are required to pay the programme fee at the time of admission to confirm their seat in the university. Below isGJUST feeand average package for top courses:
INR 30,000 - INR 1.5 lakh
Note:This information is taken from official website/ sanctioning body and is subjective.

GJUST seat intake varies course-wise. Candidates after selection need to book their seats by paying the programme fee. Availability of seats is also one of the factors determining the admission of the candidates. Below is GJUST seat intake for top courses:
Note:This information is taken from official website/ sanctioning body and is subjective.

courses_data.name: GJUST B.Ed Admission 2025
courses_data.description: B.Ed is a two-year course spread across four semesters. GJUST offers B.Ed in regular mode at the PG level. After completing graduation, candidates can apply for the B.Ed course. The selection to the B.Ed course is based on merit list prepared by the university. A pass in bachelor's degree and/ or in the master’s degree in Sciences/ Social Sciences/ Humanity with at least 50% aggregate is required to apply for GJUST B.Ed course.

faqs.question: How can I get admission to B.Ed at GJUST?
faqs.answer: GJUST shortlists candidates for B.Ed programme based onmerit in the last qualifying exam. Candidates must hold the proof of their eligibility for verification at later stages of admission. Once the selection rounds are completed, shortlisted candidates are called for counselling. Aspirants are required to lock their preferences. The list of seat allotment will be available on the official website of the university. Selected candidates need to pay programme fee to confirm their seat in the univer

faqs.question: What is the B.Ed eligibility at GJUST?
faqs.answer: A pass in bachelor's degree and/ or in the master’s degree in Sciences/Social Sciences/ Humanity with at least 50% aggregate is required to apply for GJUST B.Ed course. Candidates who have passed Bachelor’s inEngineeringor Technology with specialisation in Science and Mathematics with a minimum of 55% aggregate or any other qualification equivalent thereto can also apply forGJUST B.Edcourse.Candidates who meet the eligibility conditions are eligible to apply online for GJUST B.Ed course. Aspiran

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for B.Ed at GJUST?
faqs.answer: Candidates need to complete their bachelor's degree and/ or master's degree in Science/ Social Sciences/ Humanity with at least 50% aggregate can apply for GJUST B.Ed programme. Aspirants with Bachelor's in Engineering or Technology with specialisation in Science or Mathematics with a minimum of 55% aggregate can also apply.

faqs.question: Is entrance exam required for GJUST B.Ed admission?
faqs.answer: No, GJUST admission is entirely merit-based for B.Ed course. In case, a candidate has also passed the master’s degree examination after the bachelor’s degree, the higher percentage of marks obtained either in UG or PG degree will be taken into consideration while determining the merit. faqs.question: Which entrance exam is accepted by GJUST?
faqs.answer: Yes, GJUST conducts an entrance exam for UG and PG courses. Apart from the university-level entrance exam, the university also accepts national-level entrance exams such as CAT, JEE Main, etc. Check below the GJUST Entrance Exam details:ParticularsDetailsNo. of questions90 for UG and 100 for PGMarks per question1Duration1.5 hoursType of questionsMultiple-choice

faqs.question: When can I apply for GJUST admission?
faqs.answer: GJUST admissions usually begin inJunefor UG and PG courses with the opening of registration portal. The applications open online and continue tillJuly. For PhD courses, the application forms are usually released in thefirst week of August. The university concludes the admission within a month.

faqs.question: What documents are required for GJU Counselling?
faqs.answer: Once the candidates are shortlisted for UG or PG course based on merit or entrance exam, candidates are required to come for GJUST Counselling. The university verifies the documents of the candidates during the counselling process for final admission. Below are the documents required during GJUST Counselling:Result of qualifying examCertificates of Class 10 and Class 12Reservation Certificate (if applicable)Proper Sports certificate, if weightage claimed, issued by the Department of Sports & Youth Affairs, Govt. of HaryanaCharacter CertificateSubmission of proof of qualifying examination

faqs.question: Can I get admission at GJUST to BPharm course without an entrance exam?
faqs.answer: Yes, no entrance exam score is required for GJUST BPharm course. Hence, you can get admission without any entrance test scores. The aspirants are admitted based on their aggregate in the last qualifying examination, i.e.,Class 12 or equivalent. Candidates who meet the course-specific eligibility criteria can proceed with the application formalities.GJUST BPharm application can be filled out in online mode by visiting the university official website. Application must be submitted before the deadline. The university then scans applications for the eligibility fulfilment and selection. Shortlisted students have to take part in counselling for seat allotment. The seat can finally be confirmed on payment of admission fee.

placement_report.college_name: GJUST Placements 2025: Highest Package, Average Package & Top Companies

placement_data.title: GJU Placement Overview 2025
paragraphs: The placement details for the Class of 2025 are yet to be released. In 2024, a total of 88 companies visited the campus and plced 366 students with good packages. The average package secured by the batch 2024 was INR 3.8 LPA. The top recruiters of Guru Jambheshwar University of Science And Technology in 2024 include V VDN Technologies Pvt Ltd, Varun Beverages Ltd., Toyo Ink India Pvt. Ltd., Oji India Packaging Pvt. Ltd., HDFC Bank, Monotech Systems , etc.

Earlier , 77 companies participated in placements drive 2023 and 330 students were selected . The highest package was INR 14.75 LPA . IBM, Nagarro, Haldiram's, Amazon and many other top companies participated in GJUST placements 2023. Growth of 4% was recorded in the 2023 highest package compared with previous year. Number of recruiters that participated in 2023 increased by 22%.
